svg.title: openclaw on Azure: orchestrator + sandbox execution
svg.desc: The openclaw "brain" runs on Azure Container Apps and talks to Azure OpenAI in Microsoft Foundry over a passwordless Managed Identity token. Untrusted tool execution is delegated through a sandbox MCP server to ephemeral, Hyper-V-isolated Azure Container Apps Sandboxes, warm-forked from a snapshot in under a second, with deny-default egress, and destroyed after use. Users reach the brain from Microsoft Teams or any browser. One command, devclaw up, provisions everything from Bicep.

g.text: One cloud brain in Teams · keyless Azure OpenAI in Foundry · disposable sandboxes for execution.
Chat with the bot from anywhere
Open the link, sign in once.
Deploy from your laptop in one command.
OpenClaw gateway · orchestrator on Azure Container Apps · scales to zero
• Plans the work and calls the model itself. Keyless.
• Delegates risky execution to sandboxes through an
in-process MCP server it spawns and drives →
drives the sandboxes (aca CLI · system MI)
in Foundry Models. The model the brain talks to.
Managed Identity signs every call. No keys.
ephemeral nodes the brain runs untrusted code in
devclaw up, a thin wrapper around the Azure Developer CLI (azd).
One command provisions everything here: brain, model, sandbox group, identities, from Bicep. Re-run to roll forward.

svg.text: Microsoft Entra ID · Managed Identity · Azure Container Apps + Sandboxes · Model Context Protocol · Microsoft Foundry · Bicep
